records.ety: [L. antiquitas, fr. antiquus: cf. F. antiquit. See Antique.]

defs: The quality of being ancient; ancientness; great age; as, a statue of remarkable antiquity; a family of great antiquity.

quotes: It not your voice broken? . . . and every part about you blasted with antiquity?

defs: Ancient times; former ages; times long since past; as, Cicero was an eloquent orator of antiquity.
The ancients; the people of ancient times.
quotes: That such pillars were raised by Seth all antiquity has avowed.
You are a shrewd antiquity, neighbor Clench.

defs: A relic or monument of ancient times; as, a coin, a statue, etc.; an ancient institution. [In this sense, usually in the plural.]
notes: [In this sense, usually in the plural.]
